model_output_raw_text: {"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 8","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 16","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 32","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 64","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The current model is likely under-capacity for CIFAR-10 within 256 steps. A modest width increase in both convolutional trunk and classifier should improve representational power with low implementation risk.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"insert_after","function":"","old":"","new":"","anchor":"            ResidualBlock(channels, dropout=DROPOUT_RATE, use_bn=USE_BATCHNORM),","text":"            ResidualBlock(channels, dropout=DROPOUT_RATE, use_bn=USE_BATCHNORM),","source":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"Increase effective depth with one additional residual block in the feature trunk, a minimal architecture change that should improve representation quality under the fixed-step budget.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"OPTIMIZER = \"adam\"","new":"OPTIMIZER = \"adamw\"","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"indexing","rationale":"After capacity increases improved loss, switching from Adam to AdamW is a low-complexity optimizer-family change that often improves short-horizon generalization when weight decay is already present.","secondary_modes":["caching"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 8e-4","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"With increased model capacity and AdamW already in place, a slightly lower peak learning rate can reduce short-horizon overshooting and improve final validation loss under fixed 256-step training.","secondary_modes":["summaries"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 8e-4","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"The previous LR reduction degraded validation loss, so reverting to the prior better-performing learning rate is the safest immediate recovery step before trying new directions.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 64","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 128","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"Under fixed-step evaluation, increasing batch size lets each optimization step see more data, which can improve loss within the same 256-step budget if compute remains within limits.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 128","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 256","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"The jump to batch size 128 produced a large gain, so the next high-value probe is increasing batch size again to improve data coverage per fixed optimization step.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.5e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"With much larger batch size, gradient noise is lower; increasing learning rate is a targeted follow-up that can improve optimization progress within the same fixed number of steps.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.5e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 2e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"Performance has improved as learning rate increased with larger batch size; a further controlled increase is the most direct next probe for better fixed-step optimization.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 2e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 2.5e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"Loss has consistently improved with higher learning rates at batch size 256, so a modest additional increase is the most promising next step for fixed-step convergence.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 2.5e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 3e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"Validation loss is still improving as learning rate increases, so another small step up is the highest-probability continuation of the current trend under the same setup.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 3e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 3.5e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"The loss continues to improve with incremental LR increases at the current large-batch setting, so another small increase is the most consistent next experiment.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 3.5e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 3e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"The latest increase regressed validation loss, so reverting to the previous better-performing learning rate is the highest-confidence move before exploring other axes.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-4","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-5","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"After stabilizing LR at a strong setting, slightly reducing weight decay can improve fit in this short 256-step regime while keeping regularization present.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-5","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-5","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"The prior reduction in weight decay produced a positive gain, so testing a further modest reduction is a direct continuation to find a better regularization level for the short training horizon.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-5","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-5","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"The lower weight decay worsened validation loss, so reverting to the previously best-performing regularization value is the safest immediate correction.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 256","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 320","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"Data-per-step scaling has been the strongest lever so far, so a moderate batch-size increase is a high-upside final-phase probe without a drastic jump in runtime.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 320","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 256","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"The larger batch size regressed validation loss and increased runtime, so reverting to the prior better-performing batch size is the highest-confidence step.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.999)","new":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.99)","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"indexing","rationale":"For a very short 256-step budget, lowering AdamW beta2 can make second-moment adaptation respond faster and potentially improve final loss without changing model or runtime.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}
